mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 earnings and driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.2 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE crosses above 5.2 percent in more than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that reduces corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST does not predict SPX 20-day returns with a Rank IC exceeding 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising real rates combined with weakening consumer credit demand force equity multiples to contract sharply, generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25pp monthly alongside declining RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive equity forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly SPX forward returns show zero correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 200,000 jobs reinforces wage growth expectations and consumer spending capacity, supporting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive following months where PAYEMS adds more than 200,000 jobs.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains elevated while consumer credit demand simultaneously accelerates, it signals excessive leverage and inflation persistence, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combination of high FEDFUNDS and rising RSAFS fails to predict negative SPX 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained environment where industrial output stagnates while housing construction accelerates, sectoral rotation toward real assets and away from cyclicals reduces S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not decline when INDPRO stagnates for three months while HOUST rises, contradicting the rotation premise.
